skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build distinctive frontend components, pages, and applications with strong visual direction and production-quality implementation.
skill.summary: Design polished frontend interfaces with clear visual direction and production-ready details.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but the flagged locations are design guidance prose in SKILL.md. No executable code, cryptographic API use, network calls, environment access, external commands, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms in Design Prose
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged repeated weak cryptography patterns on SKILL.md lines that contain metadata and frontend design instructions. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption library, secret handling, or authentication logic appears in those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are natural-language design directions and YAML metadata. There is no code path or API usage that could implement weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms in Design Prose
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance patterns on lines about design quality, typography, and visual backgrounds. The skill does not ask the model to enumerate files, users, hosts, networks, environment variables, or system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines discuss frontend aesthetics only. No reconnaissance commands, operating system inspection, or data collection behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Many AI-generated interfaces look generic and lack a clear visual point of view. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with stronger typography, layout, motion, and visual identity.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection of a clear aesthetic direction before implementation.
Supports frontend components, pages, applications, and interface workflows.
Emphasizes typography, color, motion, layout, and visual details.
Encourages production-grade HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or Vue output.
Helps avoid generic AI-generated layouts, fonts, and color schemes.
Matches implementation complexity to the chosen visual concept.
limitations: Does not include executable helper scripts or bundled design assets.
Does not replace accessibility, performance, or browser compatibility testing.
Requires project context for strict brand, framework, or design system alignment.
Final implementation quality depends on the model and project stack.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Turn a product requirement into a distinctive web page or app screen with a clear visual concept.

use_cases.title: Improve a Frontend Prototype
use_cases.description: Refine a rough interface with stronger typography, spacing, color, motion, and responsive behavior.

use_cases.title: Build a Memorable Launch Page
use_cases.description: Create a polished landing page that avoids common AI design patterns and supports a clear offer.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Simple Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design and implement a responsive page for [product or purpose]. Use a distinctive visual direction and explain the main design choices briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for reusable UI pieces.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create production-ready frontend components for [workflow]. Include states, responsive behavior, and a cohesive aesthetic direction.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface and rebuild it with stronger typography, spacing, layout, color, and motion. Keep the original business goal intact.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Complete Frontend Experience
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a multi-view app or polished product surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design and implement a complete frontend experience for [audience and workflow]. Choose a bold visual concept, include expected states, and make it production-ready.

output_examples.input: Design a page for a boutique analytics tool.
output_examples.output: A refined analytics interface with a focused dashboard, expressive typography, purposeful color accents, and responsive layouts for desktop and mobile.
A polished signup experience with clearer hierarchy, better form states, smoother transitions, and visual details matched to the product audience.

output_examples.input: Create a launch page for a design tool.
output_examples.output: A memorable launch page with a strong first viewport, direct product messaging, immersive visuals, and sections that support conversion.

best_practices: Provide the audience, purpose, brand constraints, and target framework before asking for implementation.
Ask for real interaction states, responsive behavior, and accessibility checks when the interface is user-facing.
Review generated designs against your product context instead of accepting visual novelty alone.
anti_patterns: Do not request a generic modern SaaS page without audience or product context.
Do not rely on the skill as a substitute for usability testing or design system review.
Do not add elaborate animation when performance, clarity, or accessibility is more important.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components, pages, applications, and interface workflows with stronger visual direction.

faq.question: Does it include runnable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a prompt-based design guide and does not include executable helper scripts.

faq.question: Can it work with React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance mentions HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend implementation contexts.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no confirmed malicious behavior, dangerous code, or risky system access.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the product purpose, audience, framework, brand constraints, and any accessibility or performance requirements.

faq.question: Will it guarantee a complete production design?
faq.answer: No. It improves design direction, but final quality still requires review, testing, and project-specific validation.
